title: Conservation Policy Timeline
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive scrollable timeline showing conservation policy evolution from 1960 to 2030, color-coded by policy type with expandable details and quiz mode

learningObjectives: Summarize the evolution of conservation policy from national laws to international agreements
Explain how each policy addresses specific threats to biodiversity
Evaluate the effectiveness of different policy approaches to conservation
prerequisites: Familiarity with the HIPPO framework for biodiversity threats

pedagogical.teachingStrategy: Chronological exploration with interactive details and self-testing
keyQuestions: How has conservation policy evolved from domestic to international scope?
Which policies have been most effective and why?
Why has biodiversity continued to decline despite increasing policy efforts?
How do economic mechanisms differ from regulatory approaches to conservation?
commonMisconceptions: Students often assume that passing a law automatically solves the environmental problem it addresses, not recognizing the gap between policy creation and enforcement
Many students believe conservation policy is only about protecting individual species, not realizing it increasingly addresses ecosystems and habitats
Students may think international treaties are more powerful than domestic laws, when in fact enforcement is often weaker at the international level
assessmentOpportunities: Students place policies in chronological order and explain the ecological context for each
Students compare domestic vs. international policy approaches and evaluate their effectiveness
Students design a new conservation policy that addresses gaps they identified in the timeline
